name: Archlet AI Sourcing Optimization Platform API
what_it_does: AI-powered strategic sourcing optimization platform with REST API — enabling procurement teams to run RFQ/RFP events with AI-driven bid analysis, should-cost modeling, scenario simulation, and award optimization using operations research algorithms, helping category managers make data-driven sourcing decisions with full bid transparency.
use_cases: Running RFQ events and collecting supplier bids via Archlet API for sourcing workflow automation
Analyzing bids and running award optimization scenarios via Archlet API for sourcing decisions
Integrating Archlet sourcing outcomes with P2P platforms (SAP Ariba, Coupa) via API
Querying should-cost models and bid analytics via Archlet API for category management reporting
Triggering bid simulation and scenario analysis via Archlet API for strategic sourcing decisions
not_for: Tail spend automation and simple RFQs (use Fairmarkit for automated low-value sourcing)
Accounts payable and invoice processing (Archlet is sourcing-only)
Small business purchasing without dedicated category managers
best_when: A procurement category manager in manufacturing, automotive, or pharmaceutical needs to run complex multi-attribute sourcing events with should-cost modeling — where understanding fair market price and running optimization scenarios is critical for negotiation strategy.
avoid_when: You have simple commodity sourcing, no dedicated category managers, or primarily need full P2P coverage rather than strategic sourcing depth.

auth.notes: OAuth2 client credentials flow for system integrations. Bearer token auth. Organization-scoped with sourcing module access controls. Webhook for sourcing event lifecycle notifications. REST API for sourcing event management and optimization. Integration with SAP Ariba and Coupa. SAML SSO for enterprise identity. AI bid analysis uses ML models trained on manufacturing and direct materials spend.

pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise pricing; contact sales; typically based on spend volume and number of events
pricing.notes: Swiss company (Zurich). Founded 2018. Raised $10M Series A (2022). Backed by btov Partners, SIX Fintech Ventures, Verve Ventures. Operations research and optimization science foundation. Strong in European manufacturing (automotive, machinery, chemicals). Used by ABB, Bühler Group, Metso. Should-cost modeling distinguishes from simpler sourcing tools. Academic lineage from ETH Zurich optimization research. Swiss precision engineering culture applied to procurement AI.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for sourcing event management. OAuth2 client credentials auth. Enterprise documentation with integration guides. Webhook for sourcing lifecycle events. Should-cost modeling requires category-specific configuration. Swiss company with GDPR-first design and Swiss data residency option. Annual contract required before API access.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Sourcing event reads fully idempotent. Bid reads idempotent by event ID. Optimization results idempotent by run ID. Event creation requires external ID for idempotency.
known_agent_gotchas: Should-cost modeling requires category-specific data configuration — not out-of-the-box
Award optimization is computationally intensive — results are asynchronous for large events
Annual contract required — significant investment before testing production integration
Swiss company — strong data privacy but verify EU vs. Swiss data residency requirements
Manufacturing/direct materials focus — less applicable for indirect spend or services

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR and SOC2 compliant. EU and Swiss data residency. OAuth2 with scopes. Swiss company with strong privacy standards (Swiss data protection law stricter than GDPR). Supplier bid data is highly confidential — premature disclosure could distort competition. Should-cost models contain proprietary cost structure intelligence. SAML SSO for enterprise identity. Optimization algorithms are proprietary — API output only, not algorithm internals.
